issue.title: Multi-Flow streaming performance is pretty bad (maybe related to large tool call messages)
issue.body:
### Bug Description

I have a multi-flow setup where an Agent is calling another flow (that has another agent) the called flow/agent is painfully slow. I can bring it up in the playground and it's streaming abnormally slow and I have noticed that there's a single python thread running at 100%. It seems like there's some sort of spin loop happening slowing everything down. 

I'm not really able to figure out how to profile this. 

A few more details:
Flow A is primary flow with basic Chat Input, Agent and Chat Output and has Flow B connected as a tool
Flow B has the same Chat Input, Agent and Chat Output with a Python based tool connected that does return a fair bit of data. 

I thought I had seen in the process of looking at the API that while streaming it keeps streaming the same message over and over with additional data. If this includes the tool call message that message will be bigger and bigger and bigger and then when the agent starts adding to it, it's going to be humongous. 

### Reproduction

Create Flow "Tool" with Chat Input, Agent, Chat Output, connect a tool to Agent that returns a lot of data and agent instructions to parse the data
Create Flow "Test" with Chat Input, Agent, Chat Output, connect Flow "Tool" as a Tool to that agent.
Ask Flow Test about something related to tool data

### Expected behavior

It will work and be usable...

_No response_

issue.title: Cookie deletion parameter mismatch prevents proper logout in HTTPS production
issue.body:
### Bug Description

Logout functionality fails to properly clear cookies in production environments with HTTPS, causing users to be automatically re-logged in after page refresh. Users believe they are logged out **but remain authenticated**!

Cookie deletion parameters in `/api/v1/logout` endpoint don't match cookie setting parameters:

- **Set during login**: `refresh_token_lf` with `HttpOnly; Secure; SameSite=none`  
- **Deleted during logout**: `refresh_token_lf` with `SameSite=lax` (missing HttpOnly, Secure)

Browsers require **exact parameter matching** for cookie deletion. When parameters don't match, the cookie is not deleted.

### Reproduction

1. **Login and save cookies to file:**
```bash
curl -v "https://your-domain.com/api/v1/login" \
  -H "Content-Type: application/x-www-form-urlencoded" \
  -d "username=langflow&password=langflow" \
  -c cookies.txt
```
*The `-c cookies.txt` flag saves received cookies to a file*

**Response shows different parameters for each cookie:**
```
< set-cookie: refresh_token_lf=...; HttpOnly; Path=/; SameSite=none; Secure
< set-cookie: access_token_lf=...; Path=/; SameSite=lax  
< set-cookie: apikey_tkn_lflw=...; Path=/; SameSite=lax
```

2. **Logout using the saved cookies:**
```bash
curl -v "https://your-domain.com/api/v1/logout" \
  -b cookies.txt
```
*The `-b cookies.txt` flag sends cookies from the file*

**Response shows mismatched deletion parameters:**
```
< set-cookie: refresh_token_lf=""; Max-Age=0; Path=/; SameSite=lax
< set-cookie: access_token_lf=""; Max-Age=0; Path=/; SameSite=lax  
< set-cookie: apikey_tkn_lflw=""; Max-Age=0; Path=/; SameSite=lax
```

3. **Verify logout failed - try to refresh tokens using original cookies:**
```bash
curl -v -X POST "https://your-domain.com/api/v1/refresh" \
  -b cookies.txt
```
*Uses the same original cookies.txt file*

**Result**: `200 OK` with new tokens (should be `401 Unauthorized`)  
**This proves**: `refresh_token_lf` was not actually deleted due to parameter mismatch


### Expected behavior

User should remain logged out after page refresh. Session tokens should be invalidated and refresh endpoint should return 401 Unauthorized.

### Who can help?

_No response_

### Operating System

Production: Any OS with HTTPS

### Langflow Version

1.4.2

### Python Version

3.10

### Screenshot

_No response_

### Flow File

_No response_

issue.title: /api/v1/run/advanced/:flow_id fails when running with SQLite
issue.body:
### Bug Description

When running Langflow using SQLite, attempting to use the advanced run endpoint fails with an error in DB type conversion.

Attempting to run any flow returns:

```json
{
  "detail": "(builtins.AttributeError) 'str' object has no attribute 'hex'[SQL: SELECT flow.name, flow.description, flow.icon_bg_color, flow.gradient, flow.is_component, flow.updated_at, flow.webhook, flow.endpoint_name, flow.data, flow.mcp_enabled, flow.action_name, flow.action_description, flow.access_type, flow.id, flow.user_id, flow.icon, flow.tags, flow.locked, flow.folder_id, flow.fs_path \nFROM flow \nWHERE flow.id = ? AND flow.user_id = ?]\n[parameters: [{}]]"
}
```

Extracted from the JSON for better wrapping, the error is:

```
(builtins.AttributeError) 'str' object has no attribute 'hex'
[SQL:
SELECT flow.name, flow.description, flow.icon_bg_color, flow.gradient,
flow.is_component, flow.updated_at, flow.webhook, flow.endpoint_name, flow.data,
flow.mcp_enabled, flow.action_name, flow.action_description, flow.access_type, 
flow.id, flow.user_id, flow.icon, flow.tags, flow.locked, flow.folder_id, flow.fs_path 
FROM flow 
WHERE flow.id = ? AND flow.user_id = ?]
parameters: [{}]]
```

This seems to be because the string representation of the flow ID is being passed to the sqlalchemy query instead of the UUID object. This behavior is only triggered on databases such as SQLite that don't have a dedicated UUID type.

### Reproduction

1. Run Langflow using SQLite — I've reproduced both using the official helm runtime chart on k8s and with `make init` locally on `main`
2. Create any flow
3. Attempt to run that flow via the advanced endpoint `POST http://localhost:7860/api/v1/run/advanced/:flow_id`

### Expected behavior

The flow runs without error.

### Who can help?

@cbornet ?

### Operating System

Docker on k8s, locally on macOS

### Langflow Version

1.4.2, main

### Python Version

None

### Screenshot

_No response_

### Flow File

_No response_

issue.title: Component Secrets being leaked to trace logs
issue.body:
### Bug Description

### Summary
The Following refactor exposes all secrets to the downstream tracer logs (langfuse etc)
https://github.com/langflow-ai/langflow/pull/7015


### Details
Method trace_component uses inputs before call to _cleanup_inputs
Further method _cleanup_inputs fails to redact key words such as password, server_url etc

### PoC
Configuration of any tracing service example as per https://langfuse.com/docs/integrations/langflow

### Impact
_What kind of vulnerability is it? Who is impacted?_

If these logs are exposed to northbound applications, then full breach of all systems configured in langflow would occur

### Reproduction

### PoC
Configuration of any tracing service example as per https://langfuse.com/docs/integrations/langflow
observe api_key field and other related SecretStrInput objects such as database credentials
For Top level "trace" in langfuse, Both Input and Outputs

### Expected behavior

all SecretStrInput fields should be redacted, ie restore and enhance variables such as api_key to  "***" 

### Who can help?

@italojohnny as the reviewer of the commit introducing this issue given the relatively high impacts which may occur due to this issue

### Operating System

Ubuntu

### Langflow Version

1.3.0

### Python Version

3.11

### Screenshot

For Top level "trace" in langfuse, Both Input and Outputs

Azure OpenAI Embeddings (AzureOpenAIEmbeddings-u08UL): {
model: "SECRET_NOT_REDACTED"
api_key: "SECRET_NOT_REDACTED"
dimensions: ""
api_version: "SECRET_NOT_REDACTED"
azure_endpoint: "SECRET_NOT_REDACTED"
azure_deployment: "SECRET_NOT_REDACTED"
}
}

### Flow File

Any call to trace_component in https://github.com/langflow-ai/langflow/blob/main/src/backend/base/langflow/services/tracing/service.py

Causes this issue presently.

issue.title: Installation fails for python 3.13 with Poetry due to langchain-pinecone 0.2.2
issue.body:
### Bug Description

Because no versions of langflow match >1.3.4,<2.0.0
 and langflow (1.3.4) depends on langchain-pinecone (0.2.2), langflow (>=1.3.4,<2.0.0) requires langchain-pinecone (0.2.2).
So, because langchain-pinecone (0.2.2) requires Python <3.13,>=3.9

### Reproduction

Steps to reproduce:

- install poetry 2
- run `poetry init` and `poetry add langflow`
